mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month increases in industrial production signal expanding real output capacity, which raises forward earnings expectations and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the following month regardless of the prevailing macro regime.
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO 3-month change and forward 20-day SPX returns falls below 0.03 over any rolling 3-year window, or positive INDPRO momentum periods show no statistically significant outperformance versus unconditional baseline (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate crosses above a low near-zero threshold for the first time in a cycle, markets reprice the discount rate applied to long-duration equity cash flows, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter as tightening expectations build.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises above 0.50% having been below 0.25% in the prior 6 months
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered liftoff episodes, or the average triggered-period return is not significantly below the unconditional mean (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in nonfarm payroll growth and a decline in the unemployment rate jointly confirm a strengthening labor market that supports consumer spending and corporate revenue, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next month; neither series alone is sufficient because payroll gains without falling unemployment can reflect labor force re-entry rather than tightening slack.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month annualized growth > 2% AND UNRATE falls at least 0.2pp over same 3 months
failure_condition: Joint-trigger episodes fail to outperform episodes where only one of the two conditions holds, or the interaction term's marginal contribution to Rank IC is statistically indistinguishable from zero (p > 0.10) over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: During a housing upcycle regime, rising housing starts signal durable-goods and construction-linked demand expansion that feeds into broader corporate earnings with a lag, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the following quarter; the same HOUST rise carries no reliable signal outside an upcycle because it may instead reflect mean reversion from depressed levels.
failure_condition: Rank IC between HOUST 6-month change and forward 60-day SPX returns is below 0.03 when restricted to identified housing-upcycle periods, or the effect direction reverses in more than one distinct upcycle episode.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent month-over-month growth in retail sales reflects resilient consumer demand that supports near-term corporate revenue guidance, generating a modest positive drift in S&P 500 returns over the following trading week regardless of regime.
failure_condition: 5-day forward SPX returns show no significant positive skew conditional on positive RSAFS momentum (p > 0.05) across a 3-year rolling evaluation, or Rank IC stays below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: When headline CPI year-over-year growth accelerates past a threshold associated with inflation surprise risk, markets anticipate faster monetary tightening, compressing equity multiples and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns following CPI-YoY breaches above 3% are positive in more than 55% of instances, or the mean triggered return is not significantly below baseline (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate over a short window signals an abrupt deterioration in labor market conditions that precedes broad risk-off repricing, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following trading week.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises more than 0.3pp within 3 months
failure_condition: Triggered episodes show SPX 5-day forward returns not significantly below the unconditional mean (p > 0.05), or fewer than 55% of triggered instances are negative.

mechanism_narrative: During an early-cycle expansion, industrial production growth combined with an accommodative federal funds rate jointly signals reflationary conditions that are especially favorable for equities over a quarterly horizon; industrial growth alone under a tightening rate regime does not produce the same positive effect because higher discount rates offset the earnings tailwind.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 6-month annualized growth > 3% AND FEDFUNDS below 1%
failure_condition: Joint-condition episodes fail to outperform episodes satisfying only the INDPRO growth criterion, or the interaction coefficient in a regression of forward 60-day returns is not significant at p < 0.10 over any 3-year rolling sample.
